AbnormalFlag.description:
Lab abnormal indicator.

Values mirror OpenEMR ``procedure_result.abnormal`` so a derived result round-trips to the
chart without remapping (W2_ARCHITECTURE §6).
AbnormalFlag.title: AbnormalFlag

Allergy.description: An allergy reported on an intake form.

citation.description: Where on the form this allergy was read from.

confidence.description: Extractor per-field confidence (0-1). Recorded, never thresholded.
confidence.title: Confidence

reaction.description: Reaction as printed, e.g. 'hives'. Null if not given.

Allergy.title: Allergy

BoundingBox.description:
Box locating a value on a source page, in PDF points, emitted by the extractor.

Drives the click-to-source overlay (W2_ARCHITECTURE §3.3). Coordinates are PDF user-space points
(72-DPI) with a top-left origin — the exact space the overlay renders in — so the overlay maps
them straight onto the rendered page with no conversion. Boxes come from the PDF text layer
where present (already in points); the scanned fallback converts its native pixels upstream.
BoundingBox.title: BoundingBox

BoxEvidence.description:
What a located box PROVES — not where it is.

The distinction that stops a box laundering a hallucination. On a form an option's text is
*preprinted*: "Male" and "Female" are both on the page, and "Asthma" is printed whether or
not it is ticked. Boxing that text proves only that the form offers the option — the fact is
asserted by the tick. So a box over preprinted text cannot support a checkbox-derived claim,
even though the value provably "appears on the page".

Lives here, beside :class:`BoundingBox`, because a box's evidence travels with it onto
:class:`Citation`; the geometry package imports it from here (never the reverse).

Named ``BoxEvidence``, not ``Evidence``: :class:`copilot.schemas.Evidence` is the sidebar's
guideline source card.
BoxEvidence.title: BoxEvidence

ChatRequest.description: The inbound ``POST /chat`` payload for a single agent turn.

conversation_id.description: Opaque id echoed from a prior turn's response; omit to start a new one
conversation_id.title: Conversation Id

patient_id.description: FHIR Patient logical id the turn is scoped to
patient_id.title: Patient Id

ChatRequest.title: ChatRequest

Citation.description:
Machine-readable citation for one extracted fact — the W2 citation contract (§3.3).

Contract shape: ``{source_type, source_id, page_or_section, field_or_chunk_id,
quote_or_value}`` plus a native ``bounding_box`` for lab_pdf facts. Split by who fills what,
exactly as Week 1's ``SourceRef`` is: the **extractor** supplies only what it read from the
page (``quote_or_value`` and ``bounding_box``); everything that *identifies* the source is
**stamped by code**, so the model can never fabricate a provenance pointer.

bounding_box.description: Box locating the value on the page (PDF points). Required for lab_pdf facts.

evidence.description: What the box PROVES — a printed value, or a tick asserting a checkbox option. Recorded by code from the locator that found it; never model-authored.

field_or_chunk_id.description: Leave empty — the system stamps the schema field path this fact populates.
field_or_chunk_id.title: Field Or Chunk Id

page_or_section.description: Leave empty — the system fills the page number from the bounding box.
page_or_section.title: Page Or Section

quote_or_value.description: The value/text EXACTLY as it appears on the source page, copied verbatim.
quote_or_value.title: Quote Or Value

source_id.description: Leave empty — the system stamps the source document id.
source_id.title: Source Id

source_type.description: Leave empty — the system stamps this from the document's category.

Citation.title: Citation

CitedText.description: A single free-text intake value with its source citation.

citation.description: Where on the form this value was read from.

confidence.description: Extractor per-field confidence (0-1). Recorded, never thresholded.
confidence.title: Confidence

value.description: The value verbatim as printed on the form.
value.title: Value

CitedText.title: CitedText

Demographics.description:
Patient demographics captured from an intake form (PRD Core Req 2).

Each field is captured verbatim; typed parsing (date of birth → date, sex → enum) happens at
the persistence boundary, not here. Any field absent or illegible on the scan is null — never
inferred.
Demographics.title: Demographics

DocType.description:
The document types the ingestion flow supports (PRD Core Req 1).

Resolved deterministically from the OpenEMR document category, not classified by the model:
the ``Lab Report`` category maps to ``LAB_PDF``, ``Patient Information`` to ``INTAKE_FORM``,
``Medication List`` to ``MEDICATION_LIST``. The category picks the schema; the model never
decides what kind of document it is.
DocType.title: DocType

DocumentsResponse.description: ``GET /documents`` — the patient's uploaded, extractable documents (metadata only).

documents.description: Uploaded PDF/Binary documents whose category maps to an extraction schema
documents.title: Documents

patient_id.description: FHIR Patient logical id the listing is scoped to
patient_id.title: Patient Id

DocumentsResponse.title: DocumentsResponse

EvidenceItem.description:
One ranked guideline chunk in the flat evidence-snippet wire shape.

A projection of the retriever's :class:`EvidenceSnippet` onto the minimal citation contract
(source_id / section / chunk_id / text) plus the reranker ``score`` and presentation
``topic``/``source_url`` — the fields the citation card and click-to-source consume.

chunk_id.description: Stable id of the retrieved chunk
chunk_id.title: Chunk Id

score.description: Reranker relevance score in [0, 1]; higher is more relevant
score.title: Score

section.description: Section heading the chunk was drawn from
section.title: Section

source_url.description: Public URL of the source guideline
source_url.title: Source Url

text.description: The chunk text — the cited quote
text.title: Text

EvidenceItem.title: EvidenceItem

EvidenceResponse.description: ``GET /evidence`` — the ranked guideline chunks retrieved for a query.

evidence.description: Ranked guideline chunks, most relevant first
evidence.title: Evidence

query.description: The retrieval query that was run
query.title: Query

top_n.description: The effective number of snippets requested
top_n.title: Top N

EvidenceResponse.title: EvidenceResponse

ExtractionResponse.description:
``GET /documents/{id}/extraction`` — one document's strict-schema facts.

``report`` is the schema the document's TYPE selected — a ``LabReport`` for a ``lab_pdf``, an
``IntakeForm`` for an ``intake_form``, a ``MedicationList`` for a ``medication_list`` — with
each fact carrying its citation (bounding box + verbatim value) and per-value confidence.
``doc_type`` is the human-readable discriminant, resolved server-side from the document's
OpenEMR category (never a caller input).

doc_type.description: Which schema the document's category selected

report.description: LabReport for a lab_pdf, IntakeForm for an intake_form, MedicationList for a medication_list
report.title: Report

ExtractionResponse.title: ExtractionResponse

FamilyHistoryItem.description: One family-history entry reported on an intake form.

citation.description: Where on the form this entry was read from.

confidence.description: Extractor per-field confidence (0-1). Recorded, never thresholded.
confidence.title: Confidence

relation.description: Affected relative, e.g. 'mother'. Null if not given.

FamilyHistoryItem.title: FamilyHistoryItem

IntakeForm.description:
Strict-schema extraction of an ``intake_form`` document (PRD Core Req 2).

The canonical contract for intake extraction (W2_ARCHITECTURE §3.1 step 3). List sections are
always present but may be empty when the form reports none; an empty list means 'none read
from the form' — which the answer layer treats as missing-data, not as an affirmative 'none'.

No medications: :class:`MedicationList` owns them, so the two document types are mutually
exclusive. Intake persists allergies only (demographics, chief concern, family history are
display-only).

allergies.description: Every allergy read from the form, each cited.
allergies.title: Allergies

chief_concern.description: Chief concern / reason for visit as printed. Null if absent.

family_history.description: Every family-history entry read from the form, each cited.
family_history.title: Family History

IntakeForm.title: IntakeForm

LabReport.description:
Strict-schema extraction of a ``lab_pdf`` document (PRD Core Req 2).

The canonical contract for lab extraction: raw extractor output is parsed into this model at
the ingestion boundary (W2_ARCHITECTURE §3.1 step 3). Anything the extractor emits that is not
in this schema is dropped; a required field it omits fails validation and triggers a bounded
retry.

results.description: Every lab result read from the report, each individually cited.
results.title: Results

LabReport.title: LabReport

LabResult.description:
One extracted lab analyte with its citation (PRD Core Req 2 — lab fields).

Values are captured **verbatim as printed** — never rounded, converted, or inferred. Numeric
parsing for the trend widget happens downstream at the persistence boundary, not here.

``loinc`` is **optional, deliberately.** OpenEMR publishes ``procedure_result.result_code`` as a
LOINC code unconditionally, so write-back needs one and refuses a result without it (JOS-81).
But a report that prints no codes must still yield usable facts — the value, the name and the
box are what answer the physician's question — so requiring it here would turn "cannot persist
this" into "cannot read this at all", which is a much worse failure. The two concerns are
separate: extraction takes what the page offers; persistence sets its own bar.

A code that fails validation (:func:`copilot.ingestion.loinc.parse`) arrives here as None rather
than as a guess. A misread code silently mislabels *which test was run*, which is worse than no
code, because nothing downstream can tell it from a correct one.

abnormal_flag.description: Abnormal indicator; use 'no' if the report shows none.

citation.description: Where on the source page this value was read from.

collection_date.description: Specimen collection date if printed (ISO 8601). Null if absent — never infer.
collection_date.title: Collection Date

confidence.description: Extractor per-field confidence (0-1). Recorded, never thresholded.
confidence.title: Confidence

loinc.description: The LOINC code printed beside the analyte, e.g. '2823-3'. Null when the report prints none. Read it off the page — never supply a code from memory.
loinc.title: Loinc

reference_range.description: Reference range verbatim, e.g. '4.0-5.6'. Null if not printed.

test_name.description: Analyte/test name exactly as printed, e.g. 'Hemoglobin A1c'.
test_name.title: Test Name

unit.description: Unit as printed, e.g. '%'. Null if unitless/qualitative.

value.description: Result value verbatim, e.g. '8.2' or 'Positive'. Never round or convert.
value.title: Value

LabResult.title: LabResult

Medication.description: A medication read from a medication list — name plus verbatim dose and frequency.

citation.description: Where on the document this medication was read from.

confidence.description: Extractor per-field confidence (0-1). Recorded, never thresholded.
confidence.title: Confidence

dose.description: Dose/strength as printed, e.g. '500 mg'. Null if not given.

frequency.description: Frequency as printed, e.g. 'twice daily'. Null if not given.

Medication.title: Medication

MedicationList.description:
Strict-schema extraction of a ``medication_list`` document (PRD Core Req 1).

The third document type (W2_ARCHITECTURE §3.1) — a pharmacy profile, discharge list, or
reconciliation sheet. Owns medications, which ``intake_form`` no longer extracts. An empty
``medications`` list means 'none read', treated as missing-data, not an affirmative 'none'.

medications.description: Every medication read from the list, each cited.
medications.title: Medications

MedicationList.title: MedicationList

SourceType.description:
Citation source vocabulary (W2_ARCHITECTURE §3.3).

Extraction facts cite a document (``LAB_PDF`` / ``INTAKE_FORM``); the answer layer also cites
``GUIDELINE`` (RAG) and ``OPENEMR_RECORD`` (baseline chart). All four live here so the
citation contract is one shared shape across the project.
SourceType.title: SourceType

UploadedDocumentSummary.description:
A discovered uploaded document, and which extraction schema its category names.

Returned by ``get_documents`` so the intake-extractor can find a patient's uploaded documents
and hand one to ``attach_and_extract``. A document qualifies only when it has a binary
(PDF/Binary) attachment AND its category resolves to a schema — so an advance directive or a
referral is never listed, and never read through a schema that does not describe it.

``doc_type`` rides on the summary so the tool that extracts a document never has to be TOLD
what kind it is: the type was resolved from the record's own category at discovery, which is
what keeps the choice of schema out of the model's hands.

Metadata only — the document is OCR'd from its bytes, not read from FHIR (the SMART token
cannot read the Binary; see the seam spec), so no ``citation_identity`` is needed here.

doc_type.description: Which schema this document is read through

UploadedDocumentSummary.title: UploadedDocumentSummary

post.description:
Answer one agent turn, grounded and traced (ARCHITECTURE.md §6.2).

The agent run is wrapped in a Langfuse trace context; the instrumentation captures the
model, tokens, cost, and tool spans automatically, and the verification outcome is
recorded as a score. Tracing failures never affect the response.

get.description:
List the patient's uploaded, extractable documents (metadata only).

Backs the ``list_documents`` tool: the same patient-scoped FHIR ``DocumentReference``
search, filtered to uploaded PDFs whose category resolves to an extraction schema.

Extract one uploaded document into its strict-schema facts (synchronous; no LLM).

Backs ``attach_and_extract`` via the shared ``resolve_and_extract`` core: the document's
``doc_type`` is resolved server-side from its OpenEMR category (never a caller input), and
an id that is not one of the patient's uploaded documents is a 404. The response IS the
extraction result — there is no async job/status to poll.

Retrieve ranked guideline chunks for a query (the hybrid-RAG path; no LLM, no PHI).

Backs ``search_guidelines``: runs the retriever (Qdrant + rerank, or the fixture fallback)
over the non-PHI corpus. Gated by the bearer token for a uniform surface, though it reads no
patient data. Corpus indexing stays a CLI dev op — this endpoint never mutates the corpus.
Liveness probe — 200 whenever the process is alive (ARCHITECTURE.md §10).

Readiness probe — 200 only when every probed dependency is reachable.

Surfaces per-dependency status for FHIR, the LLM, Langfuse, document storage, the vector
index, and the reranker (W2_ARCHITECTURE.md §10); 503 with the same body otherwise.
